📖 FUTURE FORCE FINANCIAL LMS — User Manual
1. Overview 2. Login & Access 3. Dashboard 4. Clients 5. Loans 6. Transactions 7. Client View 8. Daily Financials 9. Reports 10. Collections 11. Legal Plaint 12. Audit Trail 13. Settings 14. Backup & Restore 15. Roles & Passwords

1. Overview

The Future Force Financial LMS is a fully self-contained, offline-capable loan management system that runs entirely in your browser. All data is stored locally in your browser's localStorage — nothing is sent to any server.

Key Capabilities

  • Register and manage borrower profiles with full personal, property, employment and guarantor information
  • Issue loans, track installment schedules and record all transactions
  • Generate daily financial summaries, payment history, loan status reports and P&L statements
  • Track collections, disbursements, attendance and portfolio health
  • Generate formal legal plaints for delinquent borrowers
  • Print official transaction receipts
  • Role-based access: Admin and Employee accounts with separate permissions
  • Full audit trail of every action performed in the system
  • Export and import data for backup and transfer
💡 Since all data lives in your browser, always export a backup regularly. If you clear browser data or switch devices without importing a backup, all data will be lost.

2. Login & Access

When you open the LMS, you are presented with a login screen. There are two access levels:

RoleDefault PasswordAccess Level
Adminadmin123Full access to all sections including Settings, Audit Trail, Company Audit and Delete Requests
EmployeeSet on first loginAccess to Clients, Loans, Transactions, Reports — cannot access Settings, Audit, or Delete functions

First-Time Admin Login

1
Click the Admin tab on the login screen.
2
Enter the default password: admin123
3
Click Login. You will be taken to the Dashboard.
⚠ Change the admin password immediately after first login. Go to Settings → Change Admin Password. The default password is known — leaving it unchanged is a security risk.

First-Time Employee Login

1
Admin must first create the employee account in Settings → Employees.
2
Employee clicks the Employee tab and selects their name from the dropdown.
3
On first login they are prompted to set their own password. The admin cannot see this password.
4
On all future logins, they use their chosen password.

Logging Out

Click the Logout button in the top-right of the masthead at any time to return to the login screen.

3. Dashboard

The Dashboard is the home screen after login. It gives a live overview of the entire loan book at a glance.

Client Balances Panel

Shows every active client with their current outstanding balance. Color-coded status indicators show whether each loan is current, in grace, overdue or paid off. Click any client row to jump directly to their Client View.

Summary Stats

Displays totals across the portfolio: total principal deployed, total interest expected, total collected, total outstanding, and overall portfolio health.

💡 The Dashboard updates in real time whenever you record a transaction or add a new loan. No page refresh needed.

4. Clients

The Clients page handles all borrower registration and management.

Adding a New Client

1
Navigate to Clients in the nav bar.
2
Fill in Personal Information: First name, Last name, Nickname, TRN, NIS, NHT number, date of birth, Digicel and Flow numbers, email address.
3
Fill in Home & Property: full address, dwelling type (Owner / Rental / Family / Renting Room / Squatting / Other), dwelling colour, material (Concrete / Wood / Mixed / Zinc) and any landmark notes.
4
Fill in Employment: employer name, work address, detailed office address, employer contact number and occupation.
5
Fill in Existing Loans Elsewhere: number of other loans, which lenders, total amount owed elsewhere and notes.
6
Fill in Guarantor: full profile — name, nickname, relationship, contact numbers, email, home address, employer, work address, dwelling description and notes.
7
Optionally fill in the Initial Loan section to issue the first loan at the same time as registration.
8
Click Save Client. A unique Client ID is automatically assigned.
💡 A Registration Fee of J$2,500 is automatically recorded when a loan is issued.

Editing or Deleting a Client

Scroll to the All Clients panel at the bottom of the Clients page. Each row has an Edit button and a Del button (Admin only). Employees see Request Del instead — this submits a deletion request for Admin review and approval.

Issuing a Loan to an Existing Client

Use the Issue New Loan — Existing Client panel. Select the client from the dropdown, enter the loan amount, interest rate, number of installments, start date and end date, then click Issue Loan.

5. Loans

How Loans Work

Every loan has a principal amount, interest rate, total due, start date, end date and a number of installments. The system calculates the installment schedule automatically.

FieldDescription
PrincipalThe cash amount disbursed to the borrower
Interest RatePercentage charged on the principal (e.g. 15 = 15%)
Total DuePrincipal + Interest — what the borrower must repay in full
InstallmentsNumber of scheduled payments to clear the loan
Grace DaysBuffer days after the end date before a loan is flagged overdue (set in Settings)

Loan Statuses

StatusMeaning
CurrentLoan is active and repayments are on track
GracePast end date but within the grace period — not yet flagged overdue
OverduePast end date and grace period — delinquent, eligible for Legal Plaint
PaidBalance has been fully cleared

6. Recording Transactions

Transactions are recorded from Daily Financials or the Client View → Add Entry panel.

Transaction Types

TypeEffect on BalanceWhen to Use
Loan RepaymentReduces balance ↓Client makes a payment toward their loan
Late FeeIncreases balance ↑Penalty charged for missed or late payment
Registration FeeIncreases balance ↑One-time fee at loan issuance (auto-recorded at J$2,500)

How to Record a Transaction

1
Go to Daily Financials or open a client in Client View.
2
Select the client and their active loan from the dropdowns.
3
Choose the Transaction Type.
4
Enter the Amount and any optional notes.
5
Click Record Transaction. A receipt number is auto-generated.
6
A Print Receipt button appears. Click it to print an official receipt for the client.
💡 Every transaction is timestamped and logged in the Audit Trail automatically.

7. Client View

The full profile page for an individual borrower. Access by clicking any client on the Dashboard or selecting from the Client View nav page.

What You'll See

  • Profile Panel — Full personal, address, employment and guarantor details
  • Loan Summary — Principal, interest, total due, amount paid, balance remaining, status and days remaining
  • Installment Schedule — Full repayment schedule showing each installment date and expected amount
  • Ledger — Every transaction recorded against this client's loan with date, receipt number, type, amount and notes
  • Add Entry — Record a new transaction directly from this screen

8. Daily Financials

The primary screen for day-to-day loan officers. Consolidates transaction entry and today's running stats in one place.

  • Record Client Transaction — Full transaction entry panel (same as Client View)
  • Today's Stats — Running totals of cash collected: total repayments, total fees, number of transactions processed
  • Daily Report — Printable summary of the day's activity
💡 Use Daily Financials as your main working screen throughout the day. It keeps running totals without navigating between pages.

9. Reports

ReportWhat It Shows
Daily ReportAll transactions for a selected date with totals
Payment HistoryFull chronological list of all payments across all clients
Loan HistoryAll loans ever issued — active, closed and paid
Loan StatusCurrent status snapshot of all active loans: current, grace, overdue, paid
PortfolioTotal capital deployed, interest earned, recovery rate
P&LIncome vs. principal deployed — net profit/loss calculation
Tax ReportInterest income summary for tax compliance purposes
DisbursementsAll loan disbursements — cash out the door by date
AttendanceEmployee attendance log
Company AuditFull company financial audit trail Admin

10. Collections

Shows all overdue and delinquent accounts — borrowers who are past their loan end date and grace period.

  • Shows client name, loan ID, total due, amount paid, balance remaining and days overdue
  • Jump directly to the client's full profile for action
  • Feeds into the Legal Plaint generator for formal debt recovery
⚠ A borrower only appears here once past the end date AND past the grace period set in Settings. Adjust grace days carefully.

11. Legal Plaint Generator

Produces a formal Jamaican court-ready document for debt recovery. Future Force Financial is automatically set as the Plaintiff. The borrower is the Defendant.

How to Generate a Plaint

1
Navigate to Legal Plaint in the nav bar.
2
Select the delinquent client from the dropdown. Only clients with overdue loans appear in the list.
3
Select the specific loan you are pursuing.
4
Click Generate Plaint. The document is produced instantly with all loan and borrower details pre-filled.
5
Review the document then click Print to send to your printer or save as PDF.

What the Plaint Includes

  • Future Force Financial as Plaintiff and registered lending agency
  • Borrower's full name and Client ID as Defendant
  • Loan amount, interest, total due, loan reference number, start and end dates
  • Statement of agreed repayment terms and the default
  • Amount outstanding at time of plaint
  • Claim for full outstanding balance plus costs
  • Signature block for company agent
💡 Always verify the outstanding balance is correct before printing the plaint. Record any last-minute payments before generating.

12. Audit Trail Admin

A complete, tamper-evident log of every action performed in the system. Only the Admin can view it.

Every entry records: timestamp, action performed, user who performed it, and relevant data (client name, loan ID, amount, etc.).

Actions logged include: client creation, edits, deletions, loan issuance, every transaction, every delete request, password changes, settings changes, imports and exports.

⚠ The audit trail cannot be edited or deleted by any user, including the Admin. It is the permanent record of all system activity.

Delete Requests

When an Employee clicks Request Del on a client or transaction, a delete request is created. The Admin sees a numbered badge on the Delete Requests nav button. Admin can Approve (executes deletion with balance rollback) or Reject the request. All decisions are logged.

13. Settings Admin

SettingDescription
Company NameName displayed across the system, on receipts and in legal plaints
Default Interest RatePre-filled interest rate when issuing new loans (can be overridden per loan)
Grace DaysNumber of days after loan end date before it is flagged overdue
EmployeesAdd and manage employee accounts. Enter name and click Add Employee. They set their own password on first login.
Change Admin PasswordRequires current password, then new password (minimum 6 characters) confirmed twice
💡 Set your Default Interest Rate in Settings before issuing any loans — it pre-fills the rate field every time and prevents manual entry errors.

14. Backup & Restore

All data lives in your browser's localStorage. To keep it safe across devices, sessions and browser updates — export regularly.

Exporting a Backup

1
Click the Export button in the nav bar (available to all roles).
2
A file named FFF_LMS_BACKUP.json will download to your device automatically.
3
Store it safely — Google Drive, USB drive, or email it to yourself.

Restoring from Backup

1
Go to Settings → Import Backup.
2
Click the file input and select your FFF_LMS_BACKUP.json file.
3
The system loads all clients, loans, transactions and settings from the backup instantly.
⚠ Importing a backup overwrites ALL current data in the system. Make sure you are importing the correct file. This cannot be undone.

Recommended Backup Schedule

  • Minimum: export once per day at end of business
  • Ideal: export after every significant batch of transactions
  • Always export before clearing browser data or switching devices

15. Roles & Passwords

Role Comparison

FeatureAdminEmployee
Dashboard✓✓
Clients (view/add/edit)✓✓
Delete clients/transactions✓ DirectRequest only
Issue Loans✓✓
Record Transactions✓✓
All Reports✓✓
Legal Plaint✓✓
Export Backup✓✓
Settings✓✗
Audit Trail✓✗
Company Audit✓✗
Approve Delete Requests✓✗
Import Backup✓✗

Password Rules

  • Admin password: minimum 6 characters. Change from Settings → Change Admin Password.
  • Employee passwords: set by each employee on first login. If forgotten, Admin must delete and recreate the account in Settings.
  • All passwords are stored as one-way hashes — never stored in plain text.
💡 If you are the only operator, always log in as Admin. Employee accounts are for staff you want to restrict from financial settings and audit data.
